what_worked_well: Second epic creation in same session leveraged all context from EPIC-081 creation — no re-exploration needed
Parallel subagent invocation with haiku model completed in ~60s (vs ~220s for EPIC-081 with sonnet) — appropriate model tier for simpler analysis
Requirements doc clearly declared CLAP as blocking prerequisite, enabling clean cross-epic dependency modeling
Locked decisions (DR-1 through DR-4) in requirements eliminated all technology/approach ambiguity
areas_for_improvement: Architect-reviewer identified unresolved reference loading mechanism — this should have been resolved in the requirements specification, not deferred to story implementation
The /create-epic command's argument parser still accepts file paths as 'epic names' (same issue as EPIC-081) — consider fixing this UX

recommendations.description: Resolve the subagent reference loading mechanism (orchestration-driven vs opt-in vs auto-load) before starting EPIC-082 story creation — add to EPIC-082 as a Day 0 design decision
recommendations.implementation_notes: The orchestration-driven approach (implementing-stories passes reference paths) is most SRP-compliant

patterns_observed: Well-structured requirements with locked decisions enable single-sprint epic creation with minimal user interaction (1 AskUserQuestion for EPIC-081, 0 for EPIC-082)
Cross-epic dependency chains (EPIC-081 → EPIC-082) benefit from being created in the same session — shared context reduces analysis cost
ai_analysis.constraint_analysis: All deliverables use approved technologies (Markdown, Grep, Read, Write). No tech-stack.md violations. Generated project-*.md files are per-project outputs, not framework source files — no source-tree.md updates needed for this epic.
